heading: 1.4 Contingency Tables (Two Categorical Variables)
text: analysing two categorical variables, use contingency table (also called two-way table cross-tabulation).Types distributionsJoint distribution: proportions cases cellMarginal distribution: proportions one variable (row column totals)Conditional distribution: proportions within subgroupAssessing associationTwo categorical variables associated conditional distributions differ groups. conditional distributions similar, variables considered independent.Graphical supportStacked bar charts useful comparing conditional distributions visually.assess association, always compare conditional distributions, just raw counts.

important features distribution.Describing quantitative dataFour key features used describe distribution:Shape – symmetry, skewness, peaks, outliersCentre – typical valueSpread – variable data areOutliers – unusually large small valuesMeasures centreMean

Resistant outliersPreferred skewed distributionsMeasures spreadRange – simple highly sensitive outliersInterquartile range (IQR) – resistant outliersStandard deviation – measures typical distance meanChoosing appropriate measuresFor approximately symmetric distributions:
Use mean centre

Use median centre

Use median centreUse IQR spreadNumerical summaries always interpreted alongside graphical displays.

heading: 2.3 Ethics and Statistics
text:
Ethical practice essential stages statistical work, data collection
analysis reporting.ethics mattersStatistical results influence decisions health, education, business, public policyPoor dishonest practice can lead misinformationConsumers statistics often access original data verify claimsThe American Statistical Association (ASA) provides Ethical Guidelines Statistical Practice support ethical decision-making statistics.Key ethical considerationsReproducibility open dataSecurity data storage data transmissionEthical treatment human animal participants, including confidentiality informed consentIntellectual propertyThe Ethical StatisticianWhen evaluating reporting statistical analyses, ethical statistician:acknowledges assumptions limitationsreports sources data sample selectionclearly identifies responsibility statistical workcommunicates findings honestly using appropriate tables, models, graphicsdiscloses funding sources intended use resultssupports replication possible protecting confidentialitycorrects errors promptly transparently discoveredEthical statistical practice just avoiding misconduct — 
building trust transparency, care, responsibility.
